title: Abort: `PyTraceBack_Here` appends to a non-traceback `__traceback__` under OOM (`traceback.c:313`)
description: Under OOM the in-flight exception's `__traceback__` (exc->traceback) holds an object that is neither NULL nor a traceback. When the eval loop appends the current frame via `PyTraceBack_Here` -> `_PyTraceBack_FromFrame`, `assert(tb_next == NULL || PyTraceBack_Check(tb_next))` (traceback.c:313) fails. exc is a valid exception instance (its own assert passes), so this is C-level corruption: the traceback was over-decref'd/freed while exc still referenced it and the storage reused as another type (use-after-free; same eval-loop over-decref class as OOM-0005). Py_DEBUG-gated assert; latent UB on release.
notes: DETECTOR assert (gh-89373-style): the defect is an over-decref/UAF of exc->traceback on the OOM unwinding path, NOT traceback.c (which correctly asserts the invariant). PyTraceBack_Here (traceback.c:326) does tb=PyException_GetTraceback(exc); _PyTraceBack_FromFrame(tb,frame); the latter asserts tb_next==NULL||PyTraceBack_Check(tb_next) at :313. exc is a valid exception (its own assert(PyExceptionInstance_Check(exc)) passes) but exc->traceback points at a live-but-wrong-type object -> the traceback was freed while referenced and reused. __traceback__ can't be set to a non-traceback from Python (descriptor type-checks) so it's C-level. Py_DEBUG-only abort (ft_debug_asan + jit); on release the assert is compiled out and the dangling tb_next is linked into the new traceback then traversed/freed -> latent UAF (matrix ft_release/upstream = n/a for this signature). Recurring across machines (local + magalu) and traceback-touching modules: inspect, _pyrepl_render, xmlrpc_server. DISTINCT from the PEP-657-caret traceback OOM finding (that is ast.parse via traceback.py caret formatting). Minimization: OPEN -- vehicle-confirmed (vehicle_source.py, target inspect), but a plain raise-through-frames-under-OOM sweep does NOT reproduce (the over-decref trigger is unisolated). DIAGNOSTIC REFINED (1b9fe5c, 2026-06-24): CONFIRMED a detector face of the eval-loop over-decref/freelist-corruption family (cluster with OOM-0004/OOM-0005), NOT an independent producer. The traceback.c:313 assert was the capture-time face; re-running vehicle_source.py now lands on sibling detectors of the same corruption: debug-ft-nojit-asan -> pycore_stackref.h:726 negrefcount 27/27 (inspect/_pyrepl_render/xmlrpc_server), gdb shows the over-decref'd object's ob_type==0xdd (already freed), decref via PyStackRef_XCLOSE in LABEL(exception_unwind) generated_cases.c.h:13857 -- a sibling of OOM-0005's frame.c:101 XCLOSE, and the statement immediately AFTER the PyTraceBack_Here(f) call (gen 13833) in the same exception-raise block; debug-gil-nojit-asan -> SEGV in tuple_alloc (tupleobject.c:48, freelist pop) via _PyTuple_FromStackRefStealOnSuccess 8/8 = OOM-0004 freelist-corruption family. So the freed object is confirmed (0xdd); the traceback assert fires when that freed/over-decref'd object is/aliases exc->traceback. Producer = shared unpinned over-decref root (same as OOM-0005). rr reverse-exec (the clean way to pin it) blocked on this Zen host by the SpecLockMap erratum (needs a root MSR change); gdb watch on exc->traceback impractical (victim varies per run). KEPT as a distinct entry so traceback.c:313 retains a dedup key, cross-linked to the cluster. Found via fusil --oom-seq OOM-injection fuzzing 2026-06-24.
